title: Miniprep Protocol Using QIAGEN
author: Aditya Mohan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8ibhuan

steps.title: Resuspend pelleted bacterial cells in 250...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pelleted bacterial cells in 250 μl Buffer P1 and transfer to a microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 250 μl Buffer P2 and...
steps.instruction: Add 250 μl Buffer P2 and mix thoroughly by inverting the tube 4–6 times.

steps.title: Mix gently by inverting the tube
steps.instruction: Mix gently by inverting the tube. Do not vortex, as this will result in shearing of genomic DNA.

steps.title: Add 350 μl Buffer N3 and...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl Buffer N3 and mix immediately and thoroughly by inverting the tube 4–6 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 min at 13,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 min at 13,000 rpm (~17,900 x g) in a table-top microcentrifuge. A compact white pellet will form. 17900 x g

steps.title: Apply 800 μl of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Apply 800 μl of the supernatant from step 4 to the QIAprep 2.0 spin column by pipetting.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 30–60 s
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 30–60 s. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00

steps.title: Wash the QIAprep 2
steps.instruction: Wash the QIAprep 2.0 spin column by adding 0.5 ml Buffer PB and centrifuging for 30–60 s. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00

steps.title: Wash QIAprep 2
steps.instruction: Wash QIAprep 2.0 spin column by adding 0.75 ml Buffer PE and centrifuging for 30–60 s. 00:01:00

steps.title: Discard the flow-through, and centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through, and centrifuge at full speed for an additional 1 min to remove residual wash buffer. 00:01:00

steps.title: Place the QIAprep 2
steps.instruction: Place the QIAprep 2.0 column in a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. To elute DNA, add 50 μl Buffer EB.
